mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent trading month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month change vs 20-day forward return is < 0.03 or negative in more than half of 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation rises above 3% year-over-year, the Federal Reserve is compelled to raise interest rates, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In episodes where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is above 50% or the average return does not differ statistically from zero at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, creating a positive tailwind for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: For months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously, the composite signal does not produce a positive average 20-day forward return significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery regimes, a strong rebound in industrial production signals that supply constraints are easing while demand remains solid, boosting corporate profits and driving S&P 500 prices higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: In recovery periods identified by NBER dating, the signal from INDPRO 3m change > 1% yields negative average 20-day forward returns or the win rate does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below an annualized rate of 1 million units signals a severe downturn in residential investment, a leading indicator of recession, and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST drops below 1.0M, the median 60-day forward return is not negative or the probability of a negative return is < 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate erodes consumer spending power and signals a weakening economy, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between UNRATE 1-month change and next 20-day return is positive in more than half of windows, or the average forward return following monthly increases in UNRATE is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate cycle, when the federal funds rate moves above its trailing 6-month average, further tightening is expected, compressing equity valuations and producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns at a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: During rising rate cycles (Fed hiking), the average 5-day return following trigger signals is not negative, or the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth is positive but retail sales growth is negative, the economy faces a supply-demand mismatch where inventories accumulate relative to consumption, signaling an impending slowdown that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months satisfying the trigger condition, the proportion of negative 60-day forward returns is ≤ 50% or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
